title: Larry Loses a Friend

jokes.text: Oh, no. Mr. Jon lovitz. Again.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Jon Lovitz is coming on the show
jokes.punchline: Darlene's exasperated reaction reveals this is an ongoing problem
jokes.explanation: Efficient character establishment through reaction. The 'Again.' adds perfect comedic timing.

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: Oh, no. Mr. Jon lovitz. Again.

jokes.text: First it was just flowers. Then it was flowers and, um... a dottie west cd. Then it was flowers and a hummel figurine. Then it was flowers and a pasta machine.
jokes.setup: Jon Lovitz has been sending increasingly elaborate gifts
jokes.punchline: The escalating absurdity from flowers to pasta machine
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with increasingly random gifts. The specificity of 'Dottie West CD' and 'Hummel figurine' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: First it was just flowers. Then it was flowers and, um... a dottie west cd. Then it was flowers and a hummel figurine. Then it was flowers and a pasta machine.

jokes.text: I think it's courageous for one man to send flowers to another man. I'm gonna put these right on the air conditioner.
jokes.setup: Hank reads Jon's note to Larry about the flowers
jokes.punchline: Hank's misinterpretation that the flowers are for Larry, not Darlene
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank obliviousness. His earnest misunderstanding drives the comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I think it's courageous for one man to send flowers to another man. I'm gonna put these right on the air conditioner.

jokes.text: Well, you just ask Paula to book him 2 more times. What would that do? Well, nothing, but by then you'd have flowers and a miata.
jokes.setup: Larry asks what to do about Jon's gifts
jokes.punchline: Hank's logic that more appearances = bigger gifts, ending with a car
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that extends the gift escalation to its logical extreme. 'Miata' is perfectly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, you just ask Paula to book him 2 more times.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What would that do?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, nothing, but by then you'd have flowers and a miata.

jokes.text: Well, 14 if you count the time we got bumped by desert storm.
jokes.setup: Jon counting his appearances on the show
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to major historical event as personal inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect showbiz narcissism - reducing the Gulf War to a personal scheduling conflict. Very Larry Sanders Show tone.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: Well, 14 if you count the time we got bumped by desert storm.

jokes.text: Fucking saddam hussein.
jokes.setup: Jon's annoyance about being bumped by Desert Storm
jokes.punchline: Personal animosity toward Saddam Hussein for TV scheduling
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity of treating geopolitics as personal inconvenience. The casual profanity adds to the comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: Fucking saddam hussein.

jokes.text: Have you seen city slickers 2? No. Well, then, put it up.
jokes.setup: Jon asks if driver has seen his movie before requesting privacy
jokes.punchline: Jon's vanity in only wanting privacy from those who haven't seen his work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment revealing Jon's insecurity and vanity in two lines. The logic is perfectly twisted.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: Have you seen city slickers 2?

dialogue.speaker: Driver
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: Well, then, put it up.

jokes.text: You're just mad because the only bracelets you have are made out of shoelaces.
jokes.setup: Office argument about Darlene keeping the expensive bracelet
jokes.punchline: Paula's cutting insult about DIY jewelry
jokes.explanation: Solid insult comedy that establishes office dynamics and economic differences.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: You're just mad because the only bracelets you have are made out of shoelaces.

jokes.text: No more comics, actors, or magicians.
jokes.setup: Darlene explaining why she won't date in show business
jokes.punchline: Including magicians in the list of show business types to avoid
jokes.explanation: The unexpected inclusion of 'magicians' with more obvious entertainment categories is a nice touch.

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: No more comics, actors, or magicians.

jokes.text: Magicians?
jokes.setup: Darlene's list of show business types to avoid
jokes.punchline: Paula's surprised reaction to magicians being included
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that highlights the oddness of the magicians reference.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Magicians?

jokes.text: I'm just... I'm nutty about irises.
jokes.setup: Larry thanking Jon for flowers meant for Darlene
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward attempt at small talk about flowers
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry awkwardness - his forced enthusiasm about flowers he doesn't understand.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm just... I'm nutty about irises.

jokes.text: Nine innings of me listening to Hank whine about his marriage bullshit.
jokes.setup: Larry describing his annual night out with Hank
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical view of what should be bonding time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - reducing a friendship ritual to an endurance test. Sets up the episode's central conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Nine innings of me listening to Hank whine about his marriage bullshit.

jokes.text: Took you a long time to figure out what I was getting at didn't it. You had a big lunch today?
jokes.setup: Larry has been hinting he wants to give away his baseball tickets
jokes.punchline: Larry's passive-aggressive comment about mental slowness
jokes.explanation: Great Larry passive-aggression. The 'big lunch' comment is a weird, specific insult that's very in character.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Took you a long time to figure out what I was getting at didn't it. You had a big lunch today?

jokes.text: I'm still getting a lot of use out of that pasta machine. Because there's nothing better than fresh pasta.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jon's escalating gifts being inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Darlene's practical appreciation for one of the weird gifts
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Darlene's practical side despite the stalker situation.

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: I'm still getting a lot of use out of that pasta machine. Because there's nothing better than fresh pasta.

jokes.text: I'm afraid poncho is no longer with us. Did he... no, he was eaten by my neighbor's rottweiler.
jokes.setup: Hank asks about missing bird Poncho
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of pet's violent death
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy with perfect misdirection. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Bird Lady
dialogue.line: I'm afraid poncho is no longer with us. Did he... no, he was eaten by my neighbor's rottweiler.

jokes.text: Oh, well, life goes on.
jokes.setup: Just revealed her bird was eaten by a dog
jokes.punchline: Casual philosophical acceptance of pet's gruesome death
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to the dark revelation. The understatement is perfectly timed.

dialogue.speaker: Bird Lady
dialogue.line: Oh, well, life goes on.

jokes.text: I don't care if my face swells up like a pumpkin. There's no way we're gonna miss that game tonight.
jokes.setup: Hank is allergic to birds but excited about the baseball game
jokes.punchline: Hank's willingness to suffer comic facial swelling for baseball
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hank's priorities. The pumpkin comparison is visual comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I don't care if my face swells up like a pumpkin. There's no way we're gonna miss that game tonight.

jokes.text: Game them! He took them. He's the fucking boss. My hands were tied.
jokes.setup: Hank asks if Larry gave away their tickets
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive escalation and blame-shifting
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-justification. The escalating defensiveness is very in character.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Game them! He took them. He's the fucking boss. My hands were tied.

jokes.text: Oh, it's not about the game. I just wanted to have some special time... ohh, what's the matter, sugar, huh?
jokes.setup: Hank seems to be having an emotional moment about missing the game
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually reacting to his bird allergies
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where apparent emotional vulnerability is actually physical discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, it's not about the game. I just wanted to have some special time... ohh, what's the matter, sugar, huh?

jokes.text: Oh, you're telling me. I've been married 5 times. They were all the fucking birds.
jokes.setup: Hank complaining about 'the fucking birds' (meaning the show's bird guests)
jokes.punchline: Larry misunderstands, applying it to his ex-wives
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay and character moment. Larry's bitterness about his marriages comes through perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, you're telling me. I've been married 5 times. They were all the fucking birds.

jokes.text: I was in a league of their own, city slickers 2, north, trapped in paradise.
jokes.setup: Conversation context is unclear
jokes.punchline: Jon randomly listing his movie credits
jokes.explanation: Classic actor behavior - dropping credits randomly in conversation. Very believable character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: I was in a league of their own, city slickers 2, north, trapped in paradise.

jokes.text: Because you got me. Tsk! We have a lot of great guests.
jokes.setup: Jon says Larry's show must be hot
jokes.punchline: Larry's deflating response to Jon's self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay. Larry's ego management vs. Jon's neediness.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: Because you got me.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Tsk! We have a lot of great guests.

jokes.text: Uh, no, no. Bruno. It's a union thing.
jokes.setup: Jon offers to help remove makeup tissues
jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd invocation of union rules for minor task
jokes.explanation: Great industry satire about absurd union rules. Perfectly delivered excuse.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Uh, no, no. Bruno. It's a union thing.

jokes.text: Aw, you forgot Mr. Hunk. No, I was gonna get to that, but to me, that comes after movie star.
jokes.setup: Hank calling Jon 'Mr. Funny' and 'Mr. Movie star'
jokes.punchline: Jon's needy correction and Hank's diplomatic ranking system
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Jon's vanity and Hank's diplomatic skills.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: Aw, you forgot Mr. Hunk.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No, I was gonna get to that, but to me, that comes after movie star.

jokes.text: she, uh... she prefers women. Oh, that's bullshit. No, she's, uh... she's a lesbian, Jon.
jokes.setup: Hank trying to delicately explain why Jon should stop pursuing Darlene
jokes.punchline: Hank's awkward euphemism followed by blunt statement
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy as Hank digs deeper into his lie. The escalation from euphemism to directness works.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: she, uh... she prefers women.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: Oh, that's bullshit.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No, she's, uh... she's a lesbian, Jon.

jokes.text: So, uh... you're a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Jon has been told Darlene is a lesbian
jokes.punchline: Jon's matter-of-fact confirmation with Darlene
jokes.explanation: Great awkward moment. Jon's casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: So, uh... you're a lesbian.

jokes.text: She's like a terrier after a rat.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Paula spread the lesbian rumor
jokes.punchline: Hank's specific animal comparison for Paula's gossip habits
jokes.explanation: Great specific metaphor that perfectly captures Paula's personality. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: She's like a terrier after a rat.

jokes.text: Like you've never been with a woman? Like you've never been with a man?
jokes.setup: Darlene is angry about Hank's lie
jokes.punchline: Hank's defensive escalation implying everyone experiments
jokes.explanation: Hank digging himself deeper with inappropriate personal questions. Classic cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Like you've never been with a woman? Like you've never been with a man?

jokes.text: There's this girl in your office I got a crush on. Darlene. Yeah. Well, how did... did she tell you? It's always darlene.
jokes.setup: Jon asks Larry about his crush
jokes.punchline: Larry's weary recognition that everyone wants Darlene
jokes.explanation: Larry's weary familiarity with this pattern is very believable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: There's this girl in your office I got a crush on. Darlene.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah. Well, how did... did she tell you? It's always darlene.

jokes.text: That would be called sexual harassment. Oh, come on! 'blow me.' That's sexual harassment.
jokes.setup: Larry explains he can't force Darlene to date Jon
jokes.punchline: Jon's ridiculous example of what he considers sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jon's complete misunderstanding of appropriate workplace behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That would be called sexual harassment.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: Oh, come on! 'blow me.' That's sexual harassment.

jokes.text: Does my ass look fat in these pants?
jokes.setup: Description of Larry's shopping behavior
jokes.punchline: Larry's typical insecure question while shopping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry vanity moment. Very characteristic behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Does my ass look fat in these pants?

jokes.text: A lot of women find me dreamy. You are dreamy.
jokes.setup: Jon's awkward romantic approach
jokes.punchline: Darlene's polite but dismissive agreement
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Darlene's diplomatic handling of Jon's ego.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: A lot of women find me dreamy. You are dreamy.

jokes.text: why would you want to go out with somebody that's famous and rich and handsome and can give you the life you always dreamed of and can make you laugh all the time? Yeah, pass.
jokes.setup: Darlene rejecting Jon for being in show business
jokes.punchline: Jon's incredulous listing of his own supposed virtues
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jon's complete self-absorption and lack of self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: why would you want to go out with somebody that's famous and rich and handsome and can give you the life you always dreamed of and can make you laugh all the time? Yeah, pass.

jokes.text: Oh! Oh! It's true!
jokes.setup: Paula sees Darlene giving back the bracelet to go to women's meeting
jokes.punchline: Paula's excited confirmation of the lesbian rumor
jokes.explanation: Paula's gossipy excitement at 'confirming' the false rumor. Good escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! It's true!

jokes.text: Thank God you weren't in here a few minutes ago when I was painting my balls.
jokes.setup: Casual comment about Larry's dressing room routine
jokes.punchline: Larry's TMI about personal grooming
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry overshare. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Thank God you weren't in here a few minutes ago when I was painting my balls.

jokes.text: God, my ass looks fat in these pants.
jokes.setup: Larry getting dressed
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier description of Larry's shopping insecurities
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Larry's vanity and insecurity.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: God, my ass looks fat in these pants.

jokes.text: Well, unless Mr. Sagan comes out and shits a string of pearls, we have no show.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about show content after Jon walked
jokes.punchline: Absurd impossible standard for Carl Sagan segment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist line. The specific image is hilarious and highly quotable. Shows Larry's panic perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, unless Mr. Sagan comes out and shits a string of pearls, we have no show.

jokes.text: she's got an eating disorder. Yeah, serious. He was gonna find out eventually.
jokes.setup: Larry apologizing to Jon on phone about Paula
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual slander of Paula to appease Jon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - throwing anyone under the bus to solve immediate problem. The casual delivery is great.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: she's got an eating disorder. Yeah, serious. He was gonna find out eventually.

jokes.text: Not like this. Emma samms. We agreed we weren't gonna discuss that. You turned on her alarm system.
jokes.setup: Discussion of romantic rejection behavior
jokes.punchline: Revelation of Larry's stalker-ish behavior with actress
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation. Larry's hypocrisy about Jon's behavior when he's done worse.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Not like this.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Emma samms.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: We agreed we weren't gonna discuss that.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You turned on her alarm system.

jokes.text: I'm a lot my character in city slickers 2. I'm very open, and I'm very honest. Very honest. That why it was so easy for me to play.
jokes.setup: Jon explaining why he's different from other show business people
jokes.punchline: Jon's self-congratulation about playing himself in a movie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jon's complete lack of self-awareness about acting vs. reality.

dialogue.speaker: Jon
dialogue.line: I'm a lot my character in city slickers 2. I'm very open, and I'm very honest. Very honest. That why it was so easy for me to play.

jokes.text: Guys, guys, and nothing but guys. That's our darlene in a nutshell.
jokes.setup: Larry confirming Darlene is not a lesbian
jokes.punchline: Larry's crude summary of Darlene's dating life
jokes.explanation: Larry's crude but effective way of dispelling the lesbian rumor. Very in character.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Guys, guys, and nothing but guys. That's our darlene in a nutshell.

jokes.text: Can you imagine darlene with another woman?
jokes.setup: After dispelling the lesbian rumors
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate final comment
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry inappropriate comment that crosses the line. Ends episode on his characteristic note.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Can you imagine darlene with another woman?

tmdb_overview: Jon Lovitz makes a strong play for a reluctant Darlene.
